SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS.justification: The algorithmic derivation is coherent and grounded in prior theory (Degris et al., 2012) — the policy gradient approximation (ignoring the ∇Q term) and the importance-weighted score function (Eq. (2)) are correctly stated and justified. Practical choices (λ-returns (Eq. (3)), clipped importance weights, and two value estimators including GTD(λ)) are sensible and adequately motivated in Sections 3 and Appendix A. However, some practical approximations introduce unquantified bias/variance tradeoffs (learning q(a|s) from an RNN, clipping ρ at 5, very small step size) that are acknowledged but not theoretically analysed, and the impact of treating the bottom LSTM as part of the environment is only empirically justified. Experimental methodology is reasonable (synthetic + real AMT labels, paired tests), but reported statistical significance is marginal in places and more analysis of sensitivity to hyperparameters would strengthen the technical claims.

ORIGINALITY.justification: The paper applies and combines existing off-policy RL ingredients (importance sampling, λ-returns, GTD(λ) value estimation) in a batch setting tailored to Seq2Seq chatbots; framing batch off-policy policy gradient specifically for RNN conversation models is a useful contribution. The components themselves are largely drawn from prior work (Degris et al., Maei, Williams), and the novelty is in their integration and application to noisy, expensive human rewards for chatbots rather than a fundamentally new learning algorithm. The paper clearly differentiates from prior on-policy Seq2Seq RL work (Section 3) but represents an incremental, applied advance rather than a radical methodological departure.

CLARITY.justification: The paper is well organized (Introduction, Preliminaries, Method, Experiments, Appendix) and provides clear equations, a pseudocode Algorithm 1, and implementation details (Section 4 and Appendix A). Notation is mostly consistent and definitions (states, actions, q, πθ, ρ, λ-returns) are explicitly given in Sections 2–3, aiding reproducibility. Minor clarity issues include occasional repeated text in the addendum and limited discussion of some practical choices (clipping threshold, hyperparameter selection), but overall the exposition is precise and sufficiently detailed for an expert reader.
